vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2156: behavior-preserving decomposition of src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.ts (1609 lines) into nine submodules under src/dashboard/server/services/conversation/, each <1000 lines, leaving conversation-service.ts as a thin curated re-export barrel.

plan.title: Decompose conversation-service.ts (1609 lines) into focused modules
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.ts is 1609 lines, over the 1000-line file-size ceiling (pinned at 1610 in scripts/file-size-baseline.txt). It bundles nine concerns: shared type shapes, provider detection, message-text filtering, session-file discovery, the core incremental JSONL parser (parseConversationMessages ~656 lines), compact-boundary scanning, context-window usage computation, activity summarization, and live file watching.
narratives.Approach: Pure relocation into a sibling directory src/dashboard/server/services/conversation/, leaving conversation-service.ts as a thin CURATED re-export barrel that preserves the EXACT current public surface (16 symbols: 11 functions + 5 interfaces). No logic edits, no renamed exports, zero call-site changes. Extraction is leaf-first so each new module imports only already-extracted siblings (or stdlib/external libs), never the barrel — avoiding runtime import cycles. Each seam is one bead, committed separately, with typecheck + lint + the FULL vitest suite green before commit. Verified facts: the file has ZERO explicit `any` (no eslint-any-allowlist edits needed); deacon-context-overflow.test.ts mocks the barrel PATH (vi.mock) so preserving the barrel keeps it green with no edit; the pi/ohmypi/codex parsers import `type { ParseResult }` from the barrel (a pre-existing type-only cycle, erased at runtime) and keep resolving via the barrel; LatestAssistantUsage is referenced by ParseResult/ParseState so it lives in types.ts; watchConversation does NOT call discoverSessionFile (so watch has no dependency on session-files). The existing src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/conversation-service.test.ts exercises the parser, context usage, activity summary, watch, gate, and discovery through the barrel and is the primary behavior-preservation oracle.
narratives.Risks: (1) Relative-import depth: new files are one directory deeper, so every `../../../lib/...` becomes `../../../../lib/...` and `./sibling.js` becomes `../sibling.js`. The compiler catches mistakes — run typecheck after each move. (2) Barrel must use CURATED re-exports (not `export *`) so internal symbols (JsonlEntry, MAX_READ_BYTES, providerFromModel, the text filters, the context-usage helpers) do NOT leak onto the public surface. (3) ModelCapability is a `typeof MODEL_CAPABILITIES[...]` value import — keep it a value import, not type-only.

autoDecisions.rationale: Issue asks for a sibling directory; 'conversation/' co-locates the split next to the barrel.

autoDecisions.choice: One module per cohesive concern = nine submodules + one finalize bead
autoDecisions.rationale: Each seam stays small and individually verifiable with full gates, per the issue's after-each-seam requirement.
Preserves the exact 16-symbol public surface and keeps internal-shared symbols off it.
It is a parse entry point that calls parseConversationMessages; grouping the three parse functions keeps the parser cohesive. It imports findLastCompactBoundary from compact-boundary.ts.
Referenced across modules (ParseResult/ParseState/parser/context-usage); a shared leaf types module avoids cycles.

items.title: Extract conversation/types.ts (shared shapes)

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/services/conversation/types.ts. Move these declarations OUT of conversation-service.ts into it: ParseResult (lines 35-82), ParseState (84-104), ConversationActivitySummary (106-119), LatestAssistantUsage (1364-1378), and the internal JSONL/limit symbols JsonlUsage (191-196), JsonlEntry (198-214), ContentBlock (216-233), MAX_READ_BYTES + MAX_FALLBACK_BYTES (188-189). `export` each of them from types.ts (the 4 internal ones must be exported so sibling modules can import them, but they stay OFF the barrel's public surface). types.ts imports only `import type { ChatMessage, CompactBoundary, ProposedPlan, WorkLogEntry } from '@overdeck/contracts'`. In the barrel, delete those declarations and add a CURATED re-export (NOT `export *`, to preserve the exact public surface): `export type { ParseResult, ParseState, ConversationActivitySummary, LatestAssistantUsage } from './conversation/types.js';`. The barrel's still-present functions import the types/limits they use from './conversation/types.js'. NOTE: ModelCapability (line 23) does NOT move here — it stays with context-usage (see extract-context-usage). After this seam run `npm run typecheck` && `npm run lint` && `npx vitest run --configLoader runner` (0 failed), then commit this bead alone. New files live in src/dashboard/server/services/conversation/ — one level deeper than the original, so EVERY relative import gains one `../`: `../../../lib/...` becomes `../../../../lib/...`, and sibling services `./codex-conversation-parser.js` becomes `../codex-conversation-parser.js`. `@overdeck/contracts` is a package import (unchanged). The TypeScript compiler is the oracle: move the block, then add imports until typecheck is green. Do NOT edit any external importer or test — they resolve through the barrel.

items.title: creates src/dashboard/server/services/conversation/types.ts (<1000 lines), which exports ParseResult, ParseState, ConversationActivitySummary, LatestAssistantUsage, JsonlUsage, JsonlEntry, ContentBlock, MAX_READ_BYTES, MAX_FALLBACK_BYTES.
the barrel re-exports exactly the 4 public types via `export type { ... }` and contains no public export of JsonlEntry/JsonlUsage/ContentBlock/MAX_*.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and the full vitest suite each exits 0; no external importer or test is edited.

acceptanceCriteria: AC1: conversation-service.ts is a thin re-export barrel (only import/export statements), materially smaller than 1609 lines, and re-exports all 16 current public symbols so every existing importer and test resolves unchanged.
AC2: src/dashboard/server/services/conversation/ contains nine cohesive submodules, each <=1000 lines.
AC3: Behavior-preserving — pure moves + re-exports, no renamed exports, no logic edits; no diff to any external importer or test.
AC4: After every seam, npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npx vitest run --configLoader runner are green (0 failed).
AC5: scripts/file-size-baseline.txt no longer pins conversation-service.ts and bash scripts/lint-file-size.sh passes.
AC6: No new explicit `any` (A1 ratchet); eslint-any-allowlist.json untouched.
